shortAnswer: Hematocrit (Hkt) indicates the percentage of your blood made up of red blood cells. A low value may indicate anemia, while a high value can suggest dehydration or certain medical conditions. Discuss any abnormal values with your doctor.
metaDescription: Hematocrit (Hkt) high or low? Normal ranges, causes, and when to see a doctor: explained clearly.
doctorQuestions: Is my hematocrit consistent with anemia?
Should I increase my fluid intake?
Do I need further blood tests?
